sentence: La ḥoguera ardía nel lar.
ipa: la xoˈɡeɾa aˈɾdia ˈnel ˈlaɾ
gloss_en: The bonfire was burning in the hearth.
notes: Pasiego aspirated Latin F-, written ⟨ḥ⟩, articulated [x] by conservative Pas speakers 'ḥoguera' (< focus) per penny2009_metaphony_iberia; 'lar' (hearth) inherited from the Cantabrian frame.

sentence: El llumiacu sube pola peña.
ipa: el ʎuˈmjaku ˈsuβe ˈpola ˈpeɲa
gloss_en: The slug climbs up the crag.
notes: Defining Pasiego trait: Latin initial L->[ʎ] 'llumiacu' (slug) retained (no yeísmo), plus ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ] 'peña' per penny2009_metaphony_iberia. NOTE: final -u 'llumiacu' is high and would metaphonically raise/centralise the stressed vowel (Penny set-2a) — DOCUMENTED, not applied by the table engine.

sentence: Ḥuegu nun ye lo mesmu que juegu.
ipa: ˈxweɡ ˈnun ˈʝe lo ˈmesmu ke ˈxweɡ
gloss_en: Fire is not the same as I-play.
notes: Conservative Pas keeps the F- reflex ⟨ḥ⟩ [x] distinct in the pair 'ḥuegu' (fire < F-) — here both map to [x] in the table engine, but the x/h contrast is documented — per penny2009_metaphony_iberia; tonic Ŏ>/ue/ 'ḥuegu','juegu'. Neuter/masculine final -u is metaphonic (DOCUMENTED, not mapped).

sentence: El pelu ta moyáu de la lluvia.
ipa: el ˈpelu ˈta moˈʝau de la ˈʎubja
gloss_en: The hair is wet from the rain.
notes: -LL->[ʎ] 'lluvia' per penny2009_metaphony_iberia; 'pelu' is exactly the metaphony minimal-pair stem (ˈpɪlʊ metaphonic vs ˈpelʊ) — the raising is DOCUMENTED but out of grapheme->IPA scope, so the engine leaves the stressed vowel unraised.

sentence: La vaca pace la yerba nel prau.
ipa: la ˈbaka ˈpaθe la ˈʝeɾba ˈnel ˈpɾaw
gloss_en: The cow grazes the grass in the meadow.
notes: Distinción inherited from the Cantabrian frame: ⟨c⟩ before front vowel [θ] 'pace' per penny2009_metaphony_iberia; falling diphthong 'prau'. The ⟨y⟩ [ʝ] of 'yerba' is the etymological /j/ reflex, not a yeísta merger (Pasiego keeps [ʎ]).

sentence: ¿Cuántos años tien to hermanu?
ipa: ˈkuantos̺ ˈaɲos̺ ˈtjen ˈto eˈɾmanu
gloss_en: How many years does your brother have?
notes: ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ] 'años' and diphthong 'tien' per penny2009_metaphony_iberia; final -u 'hermanu' is metaphonic in Pasiego (raises the stressed vowel) — DOCUMENTED, not applied by the table engine.

sentence: El gatu negru duerme cabu'l lar.
ipa: el ˈɡatu ˈneɡɾu ˈdweɾme ˈkaβu ˈl ˈlaɾ
gloss_en: The black cat sleeps by the hearth.
notes: Tonic Ŏ>/ue/ 'duerme' per penny2009_metaphony_iberia; the two masculine -u endings 'gatu','negru' are the high final vowels that trigger set-2a metaphony/centralisation of the stressed vowel — DOCUMENTED, not mapped.

sentence: ¡Cierra la cavaña, que fai fríu!
ipa: ˈθjera la kaˈbaɲa ke ˈfaj ˈfɾiu
gloss_en: Shut the cabin, it's cold!
notes: ⟨c⟩ [θ] 'Cierra' per penny2009_metaphony_iberia; ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ] 'cavaña' (cabin), tonic Ĕ>/ie/ 'Cierra' and word-initial trill [r]. 'fríu' final -u is metaphonic (DOCUMENTED, not mapped).

sentence: La ḥuente cuerre cerca la peña.
ipa: la ˈxwente ˈkwere ˈθeɾka la ˈpeɲa
gloss_en: The spring runs near the crag.
notes: Aspirated F- ⟨ḥ⟩ [x] 'ḥuente' (< fonte) and ⟨c⟩ [θ] 'cerca' per penny2009_metaphony_iberia; tonic Ŏ>/ue/ 'ḥuente','cuerre', ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ] 'peña' and trill [r] 'cuerre'.

sentence: El perru lladra tola nueche.
ipa: el ˈperu ˈʎadɾa ˈtola ˈnwetʃe
gloss_en: The dog barks all night.
notes: Word-initial L->[ʎ] 'lladra' (the defining Asturleonese-type Pasiego trait) and tonic Ŏ>/ue/ 'nueche' per penny2009_metaphony_iberia; trill [r] 'perru'. Final -u 'perru' is metaphonic (DOCUMENTED, not mapped).

sentence: La nena garra una flor.
ipa: la ˈnena ˈɡara una ˈfloɾ
gloss_en: The girl grabs a flower.
notes: Word-initial trill [r] 'garra' per penny2009_metaphony_iberia; 'nena' shows the non-metaphonic feminine -a (no high final vowel, so no set-2a raising).

sentence: Comimos borona con lleche.
ipa: koˈmimos̺ boˈɾona kon ˈʎetʃe
gloss_en: We ate cornbread with milk.
notes: -LL->[ʎ] 'lleche' per penny2009_metaphony_iberia; montañés/Pasiego 'borona' (cornbread) inherited with the Cantabrian frame.

sentence: El llobu baxa pola cuesta.
ipa: el ˈʎoβu ˈbaksa ˈpola ˈkwesta
gloss_en: The wolf comes down the slope.

sentence: Diez homes siegan la yerba.
ipa: ˈdjeθ ˈomes̺ ˈsjeɣan la ˈʝeɾba
gloss_en: Ten men mow the grass.
notes: ⟨z⟩ [θ] 'Diez' per penny2009_metaphony_iberia; tonic Ĕ>/ie/ 'Diez','siegan'. The ⟨y⟩ [ʝ] of 'yerba' is the etymological /j/, not a yeísta merger (Pasiego retains [ʎ]).

sentence: La lluna rellumbra sobre la brena.
ipa: la ˈʎuna reˈʎumbɾa ˈsobɾe la ˈbɾena
gloss_en: The moon shines over the brushland.

sentence: El mozu ḥila la lana na cabaña.
ipa: el ˈmoθu ˈxila la ˈlana ˈna kaˈβaɲa
gloss_en: The lad spins the wool in the cabin.

sentence: La ḥembra del ganáu ta preñada.

sentence: El carru ruempe la rueda na cuesta.
ipa: el ˈkaru ˈrwempe la ˈrweða ˈna ˈkwesta

sentence: El rapaz pieslla la cabaña con llave.

sentence: La xana ríe cerca de la ḥuente.
ipa: la ˈksana ˈrie ˈθeɾka de la ˈxwente
gloss_en: The water-nymph laughs near the spring.
